jokes.text: You know the story of the three little pigs, the one who built his house with bricks? That's me. I build my house with bricks. I'm the third pig.
jokes.setup: Larry defending his choice to bring a jacket to a beach barbecue
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to the smartest pig from a children's fairy tale
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry character moment - taking mundane jacket decision and elevating it to fairy tale mythology. Sets up callback for later pig references.

jokes.text: Why don't you just burn everything you eat to a cinder? Why stop at the marshmallow?
jokes.setup: Someone making s'mores around a campfire
jokes.punchline: Larry's escalation from marshmallow criticism to existential food preparation philosophy
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry misanthropy - finding the worst in innocent camp traditions.

jokes.text: I'm just saying it's idiotic what you're doing.
jokes.setup: Group having fun making s'mores at campfire
jokes.punchline: Larry bluntly calling their enjoyment 'idiotic'
jokes.explanation: Pure Larry honesty creating social discomfort. Economy of expression is perfect.

jokes.text: My jacket's not in the car. That's really strange... That's bizarre...
jokes.setup: Larry confident he brought his jacket, searching for it
jokes.punchline: The growing realization and bewilderment that it's missing
jokes.explanation: Sets up the central conflict - Larry's vindication about bringing the jacket meets mysterious disappearance.

jokes.text: That's my jacket. Marla's wearing my jacket. She took my jacket from the car?
jokes.setup: Larry searching for missing jacket
jokes.punchline: Discovery that someone else is wearing it
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the setup - goes from mystery to social violation.

jokes.text: I was taking the coat and Cheryl was mocking me for wearing a coat, 'cause it's freezing, you know. I'm not gonna take a coat... what, am I nuts? I had the foresight to bring the coat.
jokes.setup: Larry awkwardly trying to get his jacket back
jokes.punchline: Extended self-justification about his coat-bringing wisdom
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry overexplaining, creating more awkwardness. Lacks economy but perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Larry, do you want the jacket back?
jokes.setup: Larry's extended jacket justification monologue
jokes.punchline: Marla's simple, direct question cutting through the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic economy - cuts through Larry's rambling with devastating directness.

jokes.text: Wow! Wowie. What the hell is this? It might have been... I was eating a s'more... but it'll wash out, it's fleece.
jokes.setup: Larry finally getting his jacket back
jokes.punchline: Discovery that it's covered in s'more chocolate
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff - Larry gets his jacket back but it's ruined by the very thing he was criticizing earlier.

jokes.text: And I should be rewarded for that foresight, not punished.
jokes.setup: Larry arguing about the jacket situation with Cheryl
jokes.punchline: Framing bringing a jacket as deserving a reward
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry victimhood - turning basic preparation into martyrdom.

jokes.text: The pig also didn't come home to find some other fucking pig living in his house while he was locked out.
jokes.setup: Cheryl using the three little pigs metaphor against Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry corrupting the fairy tale to support his grievance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that subverts the original metaphor. The profanity and property violation make it distinctly Larry.

jokes.text: Pig? The pig... 'the three little pigs,' you don't know that story? What pig? I don't know what pig is. When did it happen? In the news? No, this is a fairy tale. Not real?
jokes.setup: Larry referencing three little pigs to his Korean bookie
jokes.punchline: Sung's complete confusion about the fairy tale
jokes.explanation: Cultural disconnect comedy - Larry's American references lost in translation.

jokes.text: Can you believe I bet the Clippers? What kind of moron am I? Have I ever won on the Clippers, huh? Never, never.
jokes.setup: Larry paying gambling debt to Sung
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of terrible betting choices
jokes.explanation: Relatable gambling futility - Larry's self-awareness doesn't prevent repeated bad decisions.

jokes.text: Boy, what are you feeding this animal? He's as fat as a house. My god, look at the size of him. Like he knows what I'm talking about. You go around calling Jeff a fat fuck. Jeff is a fat fuck. Oscar is just big boned.
jokes.setup: Larry commenting on Oscar the dog's weight
jokes.punchline: Susie's double standard between insulting Jeff vs protecting the dog
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Susie - her protective instincts are selective and hypocritical.

jokes.text: I don't get the whole dog thing anyway. It's like having a bum living in your house.
jokes.setup: Discussion about dogs and pet ownership
jokes.punchline: Larry's reductive comparison to homelessness
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical Larry observation - reduces pet ownership to its most cynical interpretation.

jokes.text: May I ask why? Considering it's none of your business, I had better use for the money. Are these pickles? They're mine!
jokes.setup: Larry confronting Heineman about not fixing his car
jokes.punchline: Heineman's aggressive territorial claim over restaurant pickles
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation from serious discussion to pickle ownership. Good economy of weirdness.

jokes.text: I told you you're feeding that dog too much. I don't feed him oreos!
jokes.setup: Oscar is dying from eating three boxes of Oreos
jokes.punchline: Larry's 'I told you so' moment vs Susie's defensive response
jokes.explanation: Larry's vindication about the dog's weight becomes tragically relevant. Dark irony.

jokes.text: Oh, the Knicks? Oh, that was a great game. Went into overtime. Yeah, they lost by four.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about game score, nurse building up excitement
jokes.punchline: Casual delivery of Larry's betting loss
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection - builds hope then deflates it. Efficient structure.

jokes.text: Sometimes you rip up these checks, it screws up the books. It can really screw up the books, these things.
jokes.setup: Larry deciding whether to keep Marla's apology check
jokes.punchline: Transparent rationalization for keeping money he doesn't deserve
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry self-justification - creating elaborate reasons for simple greed.

jokes.text: Most people aren't like you, Larry. Most people are not like you. You're special.
jokes.setup: Larry praising himself for apologizing when wrong
jokes.punchline: Quoting his mother calling him 'special' in moral context
jokes.explanation: Larry's complete lack of self-awareness while patting himself on the back for basic decency.

jokes.text: The poor dog is fighting for his life, you know, I love that dog. I mean... If anything ever happened to him, I-I don't know... I don't know what I'd do, but... I'm sure I'd figure something out.
jokes.setup: Larry expressing deep emotion about Oscar's condition
jokes.punchline: Casual undermining of his own emotional declaration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of emotional moment - Larry can't maintain sincerity even when trying to be touching.

jokes.text: Am I detecting a little... daytime sex here? Is that what this is about? I love daytime sex. Then you can just go on with the rest of the day as if nothing happen...
jokes.setup: Cheryl being affectionate and emotional
jokes.punchline: Larry reducing emotional moment to sexual opportunity
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry misreading of emotional cues - reducing everything to transactional.

jokes.text: Well, you know it's not a myth, they do eat dogs. Some Koreans eat dogs.
jokes.setup: Oscar missing, Larry suspecting Korean bookie
jokes.punchline: Larry's casual cultural stereotyping
jokes.explanation: Relies on cultural stereotype for humor - less crafted than typical Larry material.

jokes.text: I know where you live.
jokes.setup: Larry apologizing for not having cash again
jokes.punchline: Sung's matter-of-fact threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery - threat delivered as casual fact. Great economy.

jokes.text: Sung eating mysterious 'fresh meat... quite delectable... the best kind. Only the best for Sung.'
jokes.setup: Larry asking what Sung is eating
jokes.punchline: Ominous descriptions that suggest Oscar's fate
jokes.explanation: Building suspense for the reveal. Sung's evasive but enthusiastic description creates perfect dramatic irony.

jokes.text: You look like you should be pulling a rickshaw.
jokes.setup: Jeff wearing a hat at the beach wedding
jokes.punchline: Larry's culturally insensitive comparison
jokes.explanation: Relies on cultural stereotype - efficient but lazy writing.

jokes.text: Boy, I tell you something, this is one hell of a titty festival. They're all over the place. Big bosoms everywhere you look.
jokes.setup: Larry surveying the wedding guests
jokes.punchline: Crude observation about breast augmentation at plastic surgeon's wedding
jokes.explanation: Crude but observational - Larry stating obvious in most inappropriate way possible.

jokes.text: You paid for those giant fake titties.
jokes.setup: Discovering Heineman gave Larry's money to daughter
jokes.punchline: Realization that his car repair money funded breast augmentation
jokes.explanation: Good payoff connecting separate plot threads - money trail becomes absurd.

jokes.text: Less is more. I think you went a little... too big on it. It's just my opinion.
jokes.setup: Larry confronting woman with obviously oversized breast implants
jokes.punchline: Understated critique of extreme augmentation
jokes.explanation: Larry's diplomatic way of being completely inappropriate - perfect understatement.

jokes.text: They-they're festive, man. That's a festival if I ever saw one.
jokes.setup: Woman saying implants are 'festive'
jokes.punchline: Larry playing with the festival/festive wordplay
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but predictable - Larry taking literal interpretation of 'festive.'

jokes.text: It's not about subtlety, Larry, it's about excellence and confidence.
jokes.setup: Larry criticizing the obvious breast implants
jokes.punchline: Plastic surgeon defending his obviously excessive work
jokes.explanation: Good irony - professional defending obviously poor work with buzzwords.

jokes.text: Celtics lost by three.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about Celtic game score
jokes.punchline: Another gambling loss delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Consistent with pattern but repetitive - Larry's continued betting futility.

jokes.text: You have got to be kidding me.
jokes.setup: Wedding ceremony interrupted by jacket discussion
jokes.punchline: Bride's disbelief at the timing and priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction to absurd timing - bride's special day hijacked by jacket drama.

jokes.text: It's my money. I can do whatever I want with it, no? No, that was my money I gave to you to replace the soiled jacket.
jokes.setup: Larry using same logic as Heineman about the car money
jokes.punchline: Marla correcting his logic about purpose of her check
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure - Larry's hypocrisy exposed by using same flawed logic.

jokes.text: What is this dish? It's pulgoki. The korean florist brought it. It's delicious.
jokes.setup: Larry enjoying mystery meat dish at wedding
jokes.punchline: Identifying it as Korean dish from Sung
jokes.explanation: Setup for the major payoff - audience knows more than Larry about the meat's origin.

jokes.text: Oscar! Hey, pulgoki's Oscar! You're eating a do-oooog! You're eating a dog!
jokes.setup: Larry enjoying Korean meat dish, Oscar still missing
jokes.punchline: Horrifying realization that he's been eating the missing dog
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of multiple plot threads - the dark comedy payoff is expertly constructed and shocking. Classic Curb escalation.
